_comment: Synthetic contact: MEDIUM confidence Champion, holdback_group true. Source: knowledge/specs/kalder_layer2_decisions_log_L2E_builds.md § Synthetic Data Set, Contact C. Same behavioral signal profile as Contact A — isolates the holdback_group variable for measurement asymmetry demonstration.
demo_purpose: Holdback measurement control (Kohavi requirement). holdback_group: true forces the full Level 5 default experience for all module slots regardless of computed confidence_tier — experience level routing does NOT proceed normally (Document 5 §7.1–7.7). Classification scoring still runs and is stored in AEP; only experience delivery is withheld. Demonstrates measurement asymmetry — holdback contacts cannot benefit from the Tier 2 confidence upgrade.

expected_classification._note: Verified against services/scoring-engine/classifier.py classify_visitor() with signal_observations timestamps resolved to timestamp_days_ago before now (UTC), all within the last_90_days decay window (1.0x multiplier). Identical signal profile to Contact A — only holdback_group differs. Re-verify if classifier.py or kalder_data_model.py changes.
expected_classification._fallback_level_correction: Corrected from 2 to 3, same reason as contact_a.json: it_operations coverage is 'partial' at v1 (Document 5 §1.5), which caps routing at Level 3 regardless of confidence_tier (§1.3). This is the pre-holdback cascade value — Build 2's decisioning engine then applies the holdback override on top of this (Document 5 §7.1–7.7), forcing the actual served experience to Level 5 regardless of this value.
